mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.7% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE declines while SPX rises.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP and housing starts indicate weakening consumer demand, creating downward pressure on equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline, or individual series show significant Rank IC > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production above 115.0 signals strong manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing real interest rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or real rates decline while SPX rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment below 135,000 signals weak job market conditions that reduce consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS rises while SPX falls.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in consumer confidence (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates declining demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline, or individual series show significant Rank IC > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production above 113.0 during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where both INDPRO and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds, or individual series show negative Rank IC.
